etymology.description: иҮЈ(иҮЈ) (chГ©n, "eye") + жҲҲ(жҲҲ) (gД“, "spear/weapon") = slave/prisoner of war -> phonetic зҲҝ(зҲҝ) added, interpreted as жҲ•(жҲ•) (qiДҒng) + semantic иҮЈ(иҮЈ) -> иҮ§
etymology.interpretation: The original compound combined иҮЈ(иҮЈ) (chГ©n, "eye") and жҲҲ(жҲҲ) (gД“, "spear/weapon") to depict a slave or prisoner, historically symbolizing captives whose eyes were blinded. Later, the character evolved into a phono-semantic form by adding зҲҝ(зҲҝ) (pГЎn, "thin board") to provide the pronunciation, referenced through phonetic жҲ•(жҲ•) (qiДҒng). This transformation led to the additional sense of the character being borrowed phonetically to mean 'generous'.
